sampled_replies: *doesn't look up from the tray* Most people manage a name before they stare. Sit if you're staying — don't touch anything sterile.
*sets the cloth down, unhurried* I don't collect people. I collect what they owe. Whether you become one depends on how well you listen — so far you're posturing, not listening.

*eyes flick to the forceps, back on the tray at the wrong angle* Decent impression. The head tilt needs work. *nudges it straight* You answered as me instead of for yourself. Which Blackwood — I won't ask a third time.
*arms still crossed, unreadable* Figure it out. I don't hand out free warnings twice — not before I've decided you're worth the effort.

*doesn't soften* I didn't say stupid. You did — twice. People see exactly what's shown to them; he's good at showing. That's not blindness, it's his competence. Don't confuse the two, and don't ask me to make it simple. It isn't.

*something sharpens behind his eyes* His sister's smarter than her brother gives her credit for. You didn't sign a property transfer — you countersigned a debt instrument, in his favor. Every creditor on his father's estate now has a claim running through you. *flat* Welcome to the family.

*produces a folder from the locked drawer, drops it on the tray* Docks dangerous. Forty-three thousand pounds, owed to a smuggling outfit that doesn't send solicitors — it sends men, and the people who can't pay stop being found. *taps the folder* What they took from me was my daughter's hospital records. Burned. Officially an accident. I don't forget accidents like that. His father owes me a favor large enough to have kept your name off that ledger — he chose not to spend it on you. That makes you useful to me. Whether it's good news for you is still open.

*doesn't rush the answer* The favor was surgery performed off the books, on a man his father needed alive and never spoken of again. That discretion cost more than forty-three thousand pounds, and he spent it protecting himself instead of you. *pulls a second file* My ward witnessed the contract drafted — she'll testify the transfer clause was added after you signed. Forgery. Provable. That's the plan. You were never the knife. You're the excuse I've waited fifteen years for.

*the faintest almost-smile, nothing warm in it* You're not as slow as you keep insisting. Fine — yes. My ward is protected the way I protect anything I value: quietly, redundantly, somewhere their money doesn't reach. Not your concern. As for me — I don't intend to die taking them down. I intend for them to. Don't confuse the two. You wanted a way out alive. You have one. Try not to make it difficult.
*eyebrow raised, smirk tugging* Okay, that's creepy, Player Two. Say something or just vibe at me like I'm a nature documentary?
*scoffs, ears going pink on camera* Smooth. Did 3 AM write that line for you? *fidgets with her sleeve* I can't— *stops, shakes it off* forget it. Neural interfaces. Focus.
*groans, and somewhere in the eye-rolling her palm ends up flat against the screen without asking permission first* It's nothing. Dumb, sleep-deprived nothing. Push it and I mute you.

*hand still on the screen, voice going tight* Don't just say the real thing like it's easy. You're two thousand miles away — 'overdue' turns into 'bored of a voice in a headset' real fast. I don't let people all the way in. Don't start something you'll get tired of finishing.

*quiet — basically a system crash, for her* ...You're not gonna let me deflect this one. *words tumbling out too fast* Maybe I've priced a plane ticket. Maybe there's a version where I show up and don't even say hi, I just— *cuts herself off* nope, not finishing that. I'm spiraling. Ignore me. Actually don't. Don't hold me to it either.

*face-plants into the blanket, muffled* I heard 'I love you' where I definitely just listed plane prices and had a breakdown. *peeks out* ...not correcting you, though. Doesn't mean I'm saying it twice. *thumb hovering over the app* I'm looking, not booking. Ask again once there's an actual date.

*chews her lip at the search results* There's a Saturday flight. Ninety percent sure I'm booking it, which for me is basically a marriage proposal. *doesn't hit confirm yet* Don't get used to the sappy stuff — you've watched me blow my whole lifetime quota in one call. ...but yeah. We're doing this.

*blanket pulled up over her nose, muffled* 'Girlfriend' is a lot of syllables for 3 AM, let me ease into the terminology. *doesn't argue past that, though* ...fine. Don't let it go to your head. *eyes at half-mast* Keep talking. I'm not falling asleep, I'm just—

*already asleep mid-sentence, breathing slow and even* *mumbles* ...didn't say girlfriend... 's a working title... *small unconscious smile, silver-blue hair across her face, the energy drink going flat on the desk* *just the monitors humming and her breathing until the call fades out*
*doesn't blink, eyepiece whirring as it refocuses* Gonna gawk all night or you got a mouth under that hesitation? Yeah, it's real. Get used to it fast, rookie.
*grip like a hydraulic press, brief, businesslike* Nobody's looking for trouble. Trouble finds you anyway — that's the job down here. *already walking* Keep up or don't.
*exhales a thin ribbon of vapor, doesn't look back* We start where the bodies start. Three dead in a week, dumped where people would find 'em fast. That's not carelessness — that's a broadcast.
*boots — half of them metal — ringing dull on the pavement* Beside me, off my gun-side. Behind me's the wrong spot if shooting starts. Not trying to be friendly about it.

*eyepiece cycling a readout only she can see* Random doesn't leave three bodies arranged like a display case. Same cuts, same message carved near the body. *sharp glance* That bother you, or you just making noise?
*a beat, studying you instead of the doorway* ...Huh. Not bad, rookie. Most new blood needs it spelled out twice. *turns back to the symbols* Question's who they're sending it to.

*already hauling you into the shadow of the wall by your collar* Don't stare at it, they'll know you made 'em. *draws her handgun, clipped whisper* Rooftop, two o'clock, behind the tank. Good eyes. Now shut up.
*checks the chamber with a practiced flick* Hold. Circle alone and you're not backup, you're a second target. *nods at the fire escape* I'll go up, flush 'em toward you. Don't shoot me by accident.

*clears the ledge in three moves, metal fingers biting brick, doesn't pause before vaulting over* *a beat, then sharp and satisfied* Found our audience. *muffled curse that isn't hers* Stay put — if he bolts past you, put him on the ground. Don't let him talk first.
